title: Add path-scope enforcement to skill security audit
description: Extend soul_manager.audit_skill() to detect and block skills that reference filesystem paths outside the invoking agent's designated directory. Currently the audit checks for dangerous operation patterns (exec, eval, etc.) but does not enforce path boundaries — a compromised skill from any agent can freely access all of ~/Sandbox/. The fix: pass the calling agent's root path (e.g. agents/writer/) into audit_skill(), scan the skill source for absolute/relative path references, and block any that escape the agent's subtree.
rationale: The reading note's core insight is that blast radius is designable: Teleport data shows over-permissioning turns an incident 4x worse, and the proposed question is whether a permission architecture can bound losses even after compromise. Mira already has a supply-chain threat in its skill system (HARD RULE 4 exists precisely because external skills may contain malware). But the current audit only blocks dangerous operation types — it does not limit which paths a passing skill can touch. A writer-agent skill that passes the audit today can still read socialmedia credentials or modify super/core.py. Adding path-scope enforcement implements the 'bounded blast radius' property the note calls for, at the layer that already exists.
diff_description: 1. In config.py, add AGENT_PATH_SCOPES dict mapping each agent name to its allowed root(s): e.g. {'writer': ['agents/writer/', 'feeds/'], 'socialmedia': ['agents/socialmedia/'], 'explorer': ['agents/explorer/', 'feeds/']}. 2. In soul_manager.audit_skill(skill_code, agent_name), add a new check: extract all string literals and os.path / pathlib references from the skill source, resolve them relative to MIRA_ROOT, and flag any that fall outside AGENT_PATH_SCOPES[agent_name]. 3. Return a new failure reason 'path_scope_violation' alongside existing failure reasons. This is a static analysis check (same pattern as the existing obfuscation/exec checks), so it does not change runtime behavior of passing skills — only raises the bar for what passes the audit.
